question: Which customers have expired or pending KYC status?

description: PEP screening — must use both search and DB tools
question: We have a PEP-flagged transaction of NOK 2.35M to CUST-1195. What does our KYC policy require?

passages.text: Section 18. Enhanced customer due diligence measures in relation to politically exposed persons. (1) Obliged entities shall apply enhanced customer due diligence measures when the customer, any person who acts on behalf of the customer, or any beneficial owner, is a politically exposed person. (2) When establishing a customer relationship with a politically exposed person, obliged entities shall: a. ensure that senior management approval is obtained before the establishment or continuation of the customer relationship; b. apply adequate measures to establish the source of wealth and the source of funds. (4) Obliged entities shall apply the measures under subsections (1) to (3) for at least one year after the politically exposed person leaves the position or function. Source: Act of 1 June 2018 No. 23 (hvitvaskingsloven), unofficial translation by Finanstilsynet. EU AMLD6 (Directive (EU) 2018/1673) Art. 5 and EU AMLD4 Art. 23 establish equivalent requirements across the EEA.

description: PSD2 SCA compliance query — regulatory document retrieval
question: What are the PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication exemption thresholds that apply to our payment transactions?

passages.text: Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2018/389, Article 16 — Low-value remote transactions. Payment service providers shall be allowed not to apply strong customer authentication where the payer initiates a remote electronic payment transaction provided that: (a) the amount of the remote electronic payment transaction does not exceed EUR 30; and (b) either: (i) the cumulative amount of previous remote electronic payment transactions initiated by the payer since the last application of strong customer authentication does not exceed EUR 100; or (ii) the number of previous remote electronic payment transactions initiated by the payer since the last application of strong customer authentication does not exceed 5 consecutive individual remote electronic payment transactions. EBA Q&A 2018_4429: PSPs should decide at the outset which cumulative limit (EUR 100 or 5 transactions) they apply, consistently per payment instrument. Finanstilsynet supervises PSD2 compliance under the Norwegian Payment Services Act (betalingstjenesteloven).

description: GDPR data retention for transaction records
question: How long must we retain transaction records under GDPR and Norwegian AML regulations?

passages.text: Section 30. Recording and retention of information and documents. Erasure of personal data. (1) Obliged entities shall record and retain information and documents obtained and prepared in connection with measures under Sections 9 to 26 for five years after the customer relationship has been ended or the transaction has been carried out. Such retention shall take place in a diligent manner preventing unauthorised third-party access. (2) The personal data shall be erased upon expiry of the five-year time limit. Other information may be retained for longer. (4) The Ministry may in regulations lay down further rules on how information and documents shall be recorded and retained, as well as on retention of personal data for more than five years. The overall retention period for personal data shall not exceed ten years. Source: Act of 1 June 2018 No. 23 (hvitvaskingsloven), unofficial translation by Finanstilsynet. GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) storage limitation principle is overridden by GDPR Art. 6(1)(c) (legal obligation) for the mandatory AML retention period.
